metadata.description: RavenClaude — a private marketplace of opinionated Claude Code plugins. Ships ravenclaude-core (Team Lead + 14 specialists with active Structured Output Protocol + Cited-Adjudicator Escalation + alternate-methods Capability Grounding + the new `/wrap` slash command + scenario-retrieval skill for the marketplace-wide feedback loop), power-platform (11 Microsoft Power Platform specialists + bundled pbix-mcp + production knowledge bank + first plugin to enable the scenarios bank with 3 seed scenarios from the customer DEV engagement), finance (7 corporate-finance & FP&A specialists), regulatory-compliance (6 financial-regulatory specialists with field-experience BMA positioning), web-design (7 web specialists with WCAG / Core Web Vitals / SEO discipline + a curated 2026 'cutting edge yet simple' reference set), edtech-partner-success (6 PSM-lane specialists for EdTech Partner Success Manager work — K-12 / higher-ed / corporate L&D agnostic), and data-platform (4 specialists for the dashboard-engagement stack — cloud database + ELT + interactive dashboard + embed pattern — implementing the marketplace house rule 'domain plugins extend core via skills, not parallel agents' with three security skills invoked by ravenclaude-core/security-reviewer and one stack-selection skill invoked by ravenclaude-core/architect). Salesforce remains on the roadmap.

plugins.name: ravenclaude-core
plugins.description: Domain-neutral Claude Code plugin: 14 specialist agents (architect, coders, reviewers, designer, documentarian, deep-researcher, project-manager, partner-success-manager, prompt-engineer, data-engineer, etc.), team-lead dispatch playbook with cross-plugin dispatch section, format/lint/test gates, contribution-staging workflow (with security sweep + expert-routing gates), templates for memos/runbooks/design specs/RAID logs/partner-success, plugin-distributable hooks (auto-activate in consumer projects) including the new guard-recursive-spawn advisory, layout-enforcement hook + CI workflow, and slash commands `/init-agent-ready` (steers consumer repos toward AGENTS.md + CLAUDE.md + .repo-layout.json) and `/wrap` (v0.8.0 — low-friction lessons-learned capture; writes a 9-field schema-validated scenario file directly into the marketplace from any consumer session). The new scenario-retrieval skill governs how agents consult those scenarios as a secondary unverified-source bank with a mandatory unverified-scenario preamble.

plugins.name: power-platform
plugins.description: Microsoft Power Platform specialist team — 11 specialists including power-platform-tester (Test Studio, flow run-history, DAX semantic correctness, pac solution check) and power-bi-engineer (semantic models, DAX, PBIP git + Azure DevOps integration) alongside canvas/Power Fx, Power Automate + custom connectors, Dataverse modeling/security, model-driven apps, pac CLI + ALM, tenant governance/DLP, PCF, Copilot Studio + AI Builder, Power Pages. House-opinions hook covers 8 mechanically-detectable §3/§4 violations. Knowledge bank captures production lessons (currently: programmatic cloud-flow creation via Dataverse Web API when the PA Management API is blocked). **v0.10.0 — first plugin to enable the marketplace-wide scenarios bank**; ships 3 seed scenarios from the ~136-cloud-flow customer DEV engagement (spn-flow-create-403, pa-management-api-roles-null, flow-clientdata-shape-drift), with flow-engineer / dataverse-architect / solution-alm-engineer carrying inline priors that surface scenarios with the mandatory unverified-scenario preamble. Bundles the community pbix-mcp server (d0nk3yhm/pbix-mcp, MIT) for Power BI .pbix/.pbit read/write/DAX-eval without Power BI Desktop (requires `pip install pbix-mcp`).

plugins.name: finance
plugins.description: Corporate finance & FP&A specialist team — FP&A analyst (budgets, forecasts, variance commentary), financial modeler (3-statement, DCF, scenarios), controller (close, JEs, reconciliations), treasury analyst (cash, covenants, FX), valuation analyst (DCF + comps + precedent), audit-prep specialist (PBC, walkthroughs, SOC1/2 narratives), and board-pack composer. 4 skills, 8 templates, 1 advisory hook (hardcoded rates, plaintext PII, missing Sources / Assumptions). Inherits ravenclaude-core protocols.

plugins.name: regulatory-compliance
plugins.description: Financial-regulatory & compliance specialist team — AML/KYC analyst (sanctions, EDD, SAR/STR), regulatory-reporting analyst (FATCA/CRS, supervisory returns, Solvency II, BMA EBS), risk-and-controls specialist (three lines of defense, KRIs), policy & procedure writer, examination-prep specialist, and Bermuda-insurance specialist. 4 skills, 8 templates, 1 defensive PreToolUse hook (PII scrub — SSN/IBAN/credit-card/Bermuda TIN/wire instructions). Positioning reflects field experience inside a Tier-1 financial regulator (BMA). Inherits ravenclaude-core protocols.

plugins.name: web-design
plugins.description: Web design & build specialist team — web architect (IA, stack, hosting), UX designer (wireframes, flows, conversion), visual designer (brand, typography, tokens), frontend implementer (HTML/CSS/JS, React, Astro, Next), content strategist (copy, microcopy, SEO content), accessibility auditor (WCAG 2.2 AA/AAA), and performance engineer (Core Web Vitals, image/font optimization). 4 skills (design-system audit, accessibility review, Core Web Vitals tuning, technical SEO audit), 8 templates, 1 advisory hook, and a knowledge bank seeded with a curated 2026 reference set of "cutting edge yet simple" marketing-site donors (Linear, Vercel, Raycast, Resend, Cursor, v0, Tldraw, Cal.com) — visual-designer / ux-designer / frontend-implementer / web-architect all carry compact inline priors plus a pointer to the full brief. Inherits ravenclaude-core protocols.

plugins.name: edtech-partner-success
plugins.description: EdTech-flavored Partner Success Manager team — 6 specialist agents anchored on the PSM lane. 4 skills, 8 templates, 1 advisory hook, and a 9-doc knowledge bank: 3 production-lesson files (v0.2.0), 4 foundational files (v0.3.0 — CS frameworks, EdTech segments, PSM metrics, PSM tools 2026), and 2 vertical-depth files (v0.4.0 — ai-in-edtech-2026 with verified vendor pricing + COPPA April 2026 + LAUSD/AllHere diligence shift, renewal-pricing-conversations-edtech with K-12 120-180 day renewal clock + multi-year-is-K-12-exception + Recurly 71% price-increase churn data). Primary-source citations with retrieval dates and confidence notation. Vertical-explicit but segment-agnostic — fits K-12, higher-ed, corporate L&D, or mixed EdTech books. Inherits ravenclaude-core protocols; requires ravenclaude-core@>=0.7.0.

plugins.name: data-platform
plugins.description: Data-platform team — 4 specialist agents (database-setup-guide, etl-pipeline-engineer, dashboard-builder, connector-developer) that guide cloud-database setup, ELT pipelines (Airbyte / Fivetran / n8n) with connectors for QuickBooks / Stripe / Salesforce / HubSpot / GA4 / Shopify / HRIS, and interactive dashboards (Evidence.dev / Apache Superset / Metabase / Cube + Tremor) embedded in marketing sites and client apps. 7 skills — three invoked by ravenclaude-core/security-reviewer; one by ravenclaude-core/architect — implementing the marketplace house rule 'domain plugins extend core via skills, not parallel agents'. 12 templates (3 runnable security-critical + 4 conceptual + 3 seam-marked stubs + dbt-project-starter + cube-schema-starter). 1 consolidated advisory hook. 13-doc knowledge bank: v0.1.0 landscape + RLS + QBO + Power BI Embedded + EdTech LMS gap, plus v0.2.0 per-connector deep-dives (Stripe, Salesforce, HubSpot, GA4, Shopify, HRIS). Opinionated against per-viewer-priced BI tools (Looker / Tableau / Sigma) for SMB-consulting use cases; requires ravenclaude-core@>=0.7.0.
